4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The rules of search have been completely rewritten.
If your marketing team is still chasing the classic SEO playbook of the 2010s, you are optimising for a ghost town.
Generative engines (like Perplexity, ChatGPT, and Google’s AI Overviews) don’t play by the old rules. They don’t scan metadata, crawl pages, or rank keywords the way Google’s classic algorithm did.
Inside the Telemetry Engine: A Guide to AI Search Readiness
AI search has changed how people find information. It doesn’t behave like classic SEO. It retrieves meaning, not metadata. It chooses passages, not pages. It validates entities, not keywords.
Most businesses don’t realise this shift is already affecting their visibility.
The Aeobility Telemetry Engine is how we help you understand it. It’s AEObility’s diagnostic system for measuring how “AI‑ready” your content is, and where your business can improve.
Why We Built It
You want clarity. You want to know why your content is or isn’t being found. Traditional SEO tools can’t answer that. They measure rankings, backlinks, and keywords. AI systems don’t use those signals in the same way.
The Telemetry Engine evaluates your content the way modern AI systems do. It shows how well your page aligns with intent, meaning, and entity clarity. It gives you a structured, transparent view of your visibility across the Synthesised Web.
Understanding comes first. Improvement follows.
Pipeline Architecture
flowchart TD A([Input: URL + Intent]) --> B{Cache Check}
B -->|Hit| C([Return Cached Result])
B -->|Miss| D([Stage 1: Crawl & Discovery])
D --> E([Stage 2: Semantic Analysis])
E --> F([Stage 3: KG Validation])
F --> G([Stage 4: Feature Engineering])
G --> H([Stage 5: Scoring Engine])
H --> I([Stage 6: Insight Engine])
The Pipeline, Explained Simply
The engine runs a multi‑stage diagnostic on any URL and intent. Each stage builds on the last. Each output helps you see what’s working, what’s missing, and what to fix next.
Every line moves you forward.
1. Input & Caching
The engine checks whether your URL and intent have been scanned recently. If a fresh result exists, it returns it. If not, it begins a new diagnostic.
No wasted effort. No repeated crawling.
2. Crawl & Discovery
This stage answers a basic question: What’s actually on your page?
The engine extracts:

• Raw text

• Headings and structure

• Schema markup

• Core Web Vitals

• Competitors ranking for the same intent
You see your content clearly. You see the landscape you’re competing in.
Get found by understanding what you already have.
3. Semantic Analysis
This is where AI‑ready evaluation begins.
The engine crawls competitor pages and runs:

• Vector similarity scoring

• Semantic proximity checks

• RAG simulations

• Entity graph extraction
These steps show whether your content is relevant, comprehensive, and competitive. They also reveal whether an AI system would retrieve your content when answering related questions.
This is modern search behaviour. This is how AI decides what to cite.
4. Knowledge Graph Validation
AI systems rely on entity clarity. So the engine validates your entities against Wikidata.
Each entity is classified as:

• Verified

• Brand

• Emerging

• Unknown
You see whether your content is grounded in recognisable concepts or needs clearer definitions.
Clear entities build trust.
5. Feature Engineering
Here, the engine compares your content to competitors. It calculates metrics such as:

• Semantic Dominance

• Technical Advantage

• Content Length Ratio

• Coverage Gaps
These features highlight strengths and blind spots. They show where you lead and where you lag.
Structured insight creates momentum.
6. Scoring Engine
All metrics feed into a weighted model that produces a 0–100 Readiness Score. You also receive:

• Positive factors

• Negative factors

• Clear explanations for each delta
This score is transparent. You see exactly why you scored the way you did.
Confidence comes from clarity.
7. Insight Engine
Finally, the engine turns technical data into a strategic report. You receive:

• A clear Verdict

• A First Impression

• Identified Blind Spots

• A structured Summary

• Recommended Next Actions
This is where the analysis becomes practical. You see what to fix, why it matters, and how it improves your visibility.
Get found. Get chosen.
Why This Matters
AI search is rewriting the rules of discovery. Your content is no longer competing for a position on a page. It’s competing for retrieval inside an AI model.
The Telemetry Engine helps you understand:

• Why your content is or isn’t being cited

• How you compare to competitors

• What AI systems “see” when they ingest your page

• How to improve visibility across AI Overviews and generative search
It’s clarity. It’s structure. It’s a modern diagnostic for a modern search environment.
If You Want to Improve Your Visibility
Start with understanding. Start with structure. Start with a diagnostic that speaks the same language AI systems use.
